summary: The activity monitor initialization order is correctly implemented. The code already satisfies VAL-HIGH-002 requirements: activityMonitor.Start() at line 254 is called well after InitialChecks() at line 171 completes, ensuring the database is fully initialized before any DB queries are made by the activity monitor. All 19 monitoring tests pass including race detection tests.

sharedStateObservations.description: The CLAUDE.md documentation mentions 'Sync before confirm: DB writes that need user confirmation must be synchronous, not goroutines' and this pattern is correctly followed in main.go. However, the activity monitor pattern (background service starting after InitialChecks) could be documented more explicitly for future developers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: main.go:171 - InitialChecks(b) completes before main.go:254 - activityMonitor.Start()

sharedStateObservations.description: The activity monitor follows proper shutdown patterns: defer Stop() is registered immediately after Start(), and the shutdown manager also handles it. This dual-redundancy ensures clean shutdown even if one mechanism fails.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: main.go:253-255 shows defer activityMonitor.Stop() immediately after Start(), and shutdownManager.RegisterHandler at line 260-267 also handles Stop()
